message:
perf(tui): reduce frame rate to 10fps, disable effects by default, cache analytics viz

The TUI was causing excessive terminal writes and visible flashing,
especially with ambient effects enabled. The root causes were:
1. 30fps tick rate generating far more terminal output than a data
   dashboard needs
2. Bayesian diff config forced c_emit=0.0 which made every frame a
   full terminal rewrite instead of incremental diff
3. Analytics screen rebuilt its viz snapshot from DB queries inside
   view() on every frame

Changes:
- Reduce TICK_INTERVAL from 33ms (30fps) to 100ms (10fps) — more than
  adequate for a data-dashboard TUI, and input latency stays under 120ms
- Reduce toast animation ticks proportionally (entrance: 10→3, exit: 7→2)
- Reduce ambient render cadence from every 6th frame to every 2nd
  (still ~5fps animation at 10fps tick rate)
- Revert stable_tui_diff_config to ftui defaults (c_emit=6.0, dirty
  spans enabled, tile skip enabled) so the Bayesian optimizer naturally
  prefers incremental diffs over full redraws
- Disable tui_effects and set tui_ambient to "off" by default in Config
  so new installations don't pay the ambient-effect CPU tax
- Cache analytics viz snapshot in AnalyticsScreen.cached_viz, rebuilt
  in tick() so view() never performs DB I/O
- Fix macro command dispatch: execute_macro_steps and
  dispatch_palette_action_from_macro now return Cmd so side-effects
  (navigation, toasts) propagate correctly instead of being silently
  discarded
- Prevent infinite loop when a screen doesn't handle a server-dispatched
  operation by explicitly listing screen-delegated ops and showing a
  "not supported on this screen" toast instead of recursing
- Batch deferred-action and remote-event Cmds with the tick Cmd so
  their side-effects take effect in the same frame

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
